<strong>Warning:</strong> Enabling Dumb: Warning: Enabling Dumb AP mode will change this device's IP address and disable routing services. You may lose access to this UI. Only use this on mesh nodes, not the gateway.
Changing the LAN IP will disconnect you: Changing the LAN IP will disconnect you. You will need to reconnect using the new address.
Choose what appears in the header. You: Choose what appears in the header. You can display the hostname as text or use the Gulf logo.
deployment started. Check Containers in: deployment started. Check Containers in a moment.
DMZ exposes one computer completely to: DMZ exposes one computer completely to the internet. All inbound traffic will be forwarded to the DMZ host. Port forward and open port rules will not apply when DMZ is active.
If you use both VPN and IPv6 at the sam: If you use both VPN and IPv6 at the same time, it may cause IPv6 data leakage. The firewall, VPN, and some services may not fully support IPv6.
Open ports allow external access to ser: Open ports allow external access to services running directly on this router (e.g. web, SSH, FTP).
Port forwarding allows remote access to: Port forwarding allows remote access to a computer or service behind the firewall (e.g. web servers, game servers).
Set custom upstream DNS servers. Leave: Set custom upstream DNS servers. Leave empty to use the ones provided by your ISP. Common options: 1.1.1.1 (Cloudflare), 8.8.8.8 (Google), 9.9.9.9 (Quad9).
These settings control 802.11r fast roa: These settings control 802.11r fast roaming. Leave at defaults unless you know what you're doing.
<h3>How to Setup a Basic Mesh Network</h: How to Setup a Basic Mesh Network
<li>Click <strong>\"Reapply Mesh\"</stro: • Click "Reapply Mesh" to deploy the APs and activate mesh networking.
<li>Disable or delete any active wireles: • Disable or delete any active wireless networks (Go to Wi-Fi \u2192 Networks and use the toggle to disable or the "Remove Network" button to delete).
<li>Each node should have a <strong>uniq: • Each node should have a unique hostname (node2, node3, etc.).
<li>Enable <strong>Password Protection</: • Enable Password Protection, enter a Mesh Password, and click Save & Apply.
<li>Ensure all nodes use the <strong>sam: • Ensure all nodes use the same WiFi radio type (AX, AC, b/g/n).
<li>Ensure you select the <strong>same W: • Ensure you select the same WiFi radio type (AX, AC, b/g/n) for both WiFi SSID and Mesh SSID.
<li>Enter the <strong>same WiFi SSID, Me: • Enter the same WiFi SSID, Mesh SSID, and Password as the server.
<li>Enter your <strong>WiFi SSID</strong: • Enter your WiFi SSID \u2014 this is the main Wi-Fi network all devices will connect to.
<li>Go to <em>Network \\u2192 Interfaces: • Go to Network \u2192 Interfaces to confirm the Batman (bat0) device and mesh_batman interface were added.
<li>Go to <em>Wi-Fi \\u2192 Networks</em: • Go to Wi-Fi \u2192 Networks to check that the Wi-Fi networks were added.
<li>Scroll to <strong>Dumb AP / Node Mod: • Scroll to Dumb AP / Node Mode and select a hostname (e.g. node2, node3).
<li>Select the <strong>Mesh Backhaul Rad: • Select the Mesh Backhaul Radio and enter a separate Mesh SSID. (The system will automatically append -mesh to your mesh SSID.)
<li>Select the <strong>Regular AP Radio<: • Select the Regular AP Radio. (Recommended: Use a different radio than the mesh backhaul for best performance.)
<li>Set IP Mode to <strong>DHCP</strong>: • Set IP Mode to DHCP (recommended) or configure a Static IP in the same range as your Mesh Server.
<li>Use the <strong>same WiFi SSID, Mesh: • Use the same WiFi SSID, Mesh SSID, and Password for every node.
<option value=\"wan\">wan (Remote VPN IP: <option value="wan">wan (Remote VPN IP)</option><option value="lan">lan (Local VPN Gateway)</option>
<span><strong>wan</strong> = routes all: wan = routes all traffic through VPN  |  lan = VPN acts as local gateway
<strong>Important:</strong> Upload a com: Important: Upload a compatible firmware image (.bin or .img) for your device. Do not power off the router during the upgrade process.
<strong>Network isolation</strong><br>Gu:
Network isolation
Guest clients will be placed on a separate subnet (192.168.4.x) with firewall rules blocking access to your LAN. Client-to-client isolation will also be enabled.
Activate mesh Wi-Fi using batman-adv on: Activate mesh Wi-Fi using batman-adv on this router
Advanced proxy and tunneling platform. R: Advanced proxy and tunneling platform. Required for TorGuard V2Ray connections.
All available connection types are alrea: All available connection types are already configured.
All wireless networks are turned off: All wireless networks are turned off
Apps will be accessible at appname.yourd: Apps will be accessible at appname.yourdomain.com. Requires DNS A record pointing to your WireGuard public IP and TorGuard port forwarding for ports 80 & 443.
Automatically add suspicious IPs from lo: Automatically add suspicious IPs from logs to the local blocklist
Automatically allow your uplink IPs and: Automatically allow your uplink IPs and resolved domains

Caddy handles automatic HTTPS (Let's Enc: Caddy handles automatic HTTPS (Let's Encrypt) and reverse proxies all deployed apps. It listens on ports 8080 (HTTP) and 4443 (HTTPS), which should be port-forwarded from 80/443 via TorGuard WireGuard.
Change the password used to log in to th: Change the password used to log in to this router
Choose how deployed apps are accessed. L: Choose how deployed apps are accessed. Local = LAN IP only. VPN = via WireGuard tunnel. Public = exposed via reverse proxy with automatic HTTPS.
